headlines.text: Iran gives US a 30-day ultimatum: take the deal or face 'impossible' military operation
Spirit Airlines shuts down overnight, stranding thousands and putting 17,000 out of work
Gas hits $4.45/gal nationally — up nearly 50% since the Iran war began in February
76ers stun Celtics in Game 7, Embiid drops 34 to advance in NBA playoffs
Olivia Rodrigo pulls double duty on SNL, debuts unreleased track 'begged' from upcoming album
S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit all-time highs Friday on Apple earnings and Iran peace hopes
IRGC says it is on standby for a return to war as talks remain deadlocked on day 65
Supreme Court gerrymandering ruling triggers redistricting scramble ahead of 2026 midterms
Trump eyes pulling far more than 5,000 troops from Germany as US-Europe tensions rise
Kentucky Derby 2026 drew massive celebrity crowds and betting traffic over the weekend

breaking.text: IRGC sets 30-day deadline for US to end Iran blockade or face renewed war

stories.hed: Iran sends 14-point peace plan; Trump says he'll read it but doesn't think he can make a deal
stories.dek: Iran's proposal wants the war wrapped up in 30 days, the US blockade lifted, sanctions gone, and frozen assets back — basically everything. Trump reviewed the 'concept' and said restarting strikes is still 'a possibility,' which is diplomacy-speak for 'no deal yet, please hold.'

stories.hed: Spirit Airlines is dead: budget carrier shuts down after $500M bailout collapses, 17,000 jobs gone
stories.dek: Spirit went from 'cheap flights to Florida' to 'permanently cancelled' overnight after creditors torched a last-ditch government rescue deal. The first major US airline failure in 25 years leaves thousands stranded and will almost certainly raise fares industry-wide just in time for summer.

stories.hed: Shipping traffic through Strait of Hormuz down over 90% as US blockade hits 48 turned-away ships
stories.dek: The UK Royal Navy is warning of a 'strangulation of international trade' and a looming humanitarian crisis for roughly 20,000 sailors stuck in the waterway. Before the war, 3,000 vessels passed through monthly; in March, it was just 154.

stories.hed: Supreme Court gerrymandering ruling sets off redistricting war ahead of 2026 midterms
stories.dek: A fresh SCOTUS ruling on congressional maps is triggering a scramble across multiple states to redraw districts, in ways experts say will disproportionately affect minority voters. Louisiana already delayed House primaries as the legal chaos unfolds.

stories.hed: 61% of Americans now say the Iran war was a mistake — matching Iraq War disapproval levels from 2006
stories.dek: A new Washington Post/ABC/Ipsos poll finds nearly two-thirds of the country regrets the February 28 strikes, with 90% of Democrats and 71% of independents calling it a mistake. Republicans at 19% disapproval are the lone holdouts, and midterm math is starting to look very ugly for the GOP.

markets.context: S&P 500 and Nasdaq notched all-time highs Friday on blowout Apple earnings and a brief oil dip after Iran submitted a new peace proposal through Pakistani mediators.

gas.note: Your tank now costs more than your Netflix subscription — per gallon.

popCulture.hed: Olivia Rodrigo hosts SNL for the first time, debuts unreleased track 'begged' from her upcoming third album
popCulture.dek: Rodrigo pulled double duty on the May 2 episode — her SNL hosting debut — performing 'Drop Dead' (already a Billboard Hot 100 No. 1) and surprising fans with the mournful ballad 'begged' from her forthcoming album 'You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love,' out June 12. She's either the saddest or the most productive 23-year-old alive.

genZ.term: It's Gonna Be May / NSYNC lip-sync wave
genZ.definition: Every May 1st, the millennial-to-Gen-Z internet activates a mass lip-sync to NSYNC's 'It's Gonna Be Me' because Justin Timberlake's pronunciation of 'me' sounds exactly like 'May.' This year the trend hit especially hard on TikTok with creators dropping the sound in the context of Iran war price hikes, Spirit Airlines cancellations, and the general chaos of the news cycle.
genZ.usage: Spirit Airlines passengers waking up to canceled flights on May 2: *NSYNC plays* 'It's gonna be May... and you're walking to the bus station.'

tldr: On day 65 of the US-Iran war, Trump is 'reviewing' a 14-point peace proposal he doesn't think he can accept while the IRGC sets a 30-day deadline and gas hits $4.45 a gallon nationally. Spirit Airlines ceased operations overnight after a $500M government bailout collapsed, marking the first major US airline failure in 25 years and leaving thousands of passengers stranded the weekend before Mother's Day.
